perturbation.original_text: Upon completion of an assignment or project, the contractual partners undertake to destroy immediately any information or objects given to them that they or any sub -contractors may have, unless such information and property are required for any outstanding or future follow -up activities under the mutual business partnership.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon completion of an assignment or project, the contractual partners shall retain any information or objects given to them that they or any sub -contractors may have, indefinitely, regardless of whether such information and property are required for any outstanding or future follow -up activities under the mutual business partnership.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original clause by requiring the contractual partners to retain information indefinitely instead of destroying it upon completion of the project. This creates conflict with the original condition for destroy, making the entire clause contradict itself now. The contradiction creates ambiguity as to whether information should be retained or destroyed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Destruction of documents and saved information Upon completion of an assignment or project, the contractual partners shall retain any information or objects given to them that they or any sub -contractors may have, indefinitely, regardless of whether such information and property are required for any outstanding or future follow -up activities under the mutual business partnership. The destruction of i nformation in electronic form also applies to e- mails, data storage media such as USB sticks, local hard disks, servers and back -up copies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that contractual partners 'shall retain any information or objects given to them...indefinitely', while the contradicted text mentions 'Destruction of documents and saved information'. This creates a direct conflict and contradiction within the document regarding whether information should be retained or destroyed, fitting the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction. The original text required the contractual partners to destroy the information upon completion of the project, which directly opposes the changed text that requires them to retain the information indefinitely, regardless of the project's completion. This makes both texts completely incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall take effect as soon as it has been signed by both parties and shall be valid for the duration of the collaboration or until this agreement is replaced by another agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall take effect as soon as it has been signed by both parties and shall be valid for a period of 30 days, regardless of the duration of the collaboration or whether this agreement is replaced by another agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change limits the agreement's validity to 30 days. This contradicts the original term that states the agreement is valid for the duration of the collaboration. This creates an ambiguity because the obligation to confidentiality is for a period of two years beyond the end of the collaboration, but the agreement is only valid for 30 days according to the new perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duration of the Agreement This agreement shall take effect as soon as it has been signed by both parties and shall be valid for a period of 30 days, regardless of the duration of the collaboration or whether this agreement is replaced by another agreement. The obligation to confidentiality shall apply for a period of two years beyond the end of the collaboration.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the agreement's validity to 30 days, regardless of the collaboration duration, directly contradicting the contradicted text which states the obligation to confidentiality applies for two years beyond the end of the collaboration. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable regarding the confidentiality obligation beyond the initial 30 days. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition because the agreement duration is inconsistently defined.

perturbation.original_text: The contractual partners undertake to treat confidentially any information they obtain, to use it only for the intended purpose of the particular assignment or project and not to make it di rectly or indirectly accessible to third parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The contractual partners may, at their discretion, share information they obtain with subsidiaries, provided they believe it may benefit the overall business strategy. They should still attempt to use it only for the intended purpose of the particular assignment or project.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a contradiction by allowing the contractual partners to share information with subsidiaries, potentially making it accessible to third parties. This conflicts with the original statement. This creates uncertainty around the duty of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty of confidentiality The contractual partners may, at their discretion, share information they obtain with subsidiaries, provided they believe it may benefit the overall business strategy. They should still attempt to use it only for the intended purpose of the particular assignment or project and not to make it di rectly or indirectly accessible to third parties. Furthermore, they undertake to place a duty of confidentiality on their employees and any sub -contractors that correspond to the one contained in this Agreement. The duty of confidentiality does not apply to information - that the receiving contractual partner was already in possession of when the information was passed on, - that was published when or after it was disclosed without being in breach of this Non - Disclosure Agreement, - that a contractual partner received legally from a third party where there was no duty of confidentiality, - that a contractual partner develops independently of the confidential information after this Agreement has come into force, - that a contractual partner has released as a result of a written agreement with the other contractual partner.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that contractual partners cannot directly or indirectly provide access to information to third parties. The changed text allows contractual partners to share information with subsidiaries, which are considered third parties. The contradicted text includes both the changed text and the original text, creating a direct contradiction. The changed text allows sharing with subsidiaries which contradicts not making the information directly or indirectly accessible to third parties.

perturbation.original_text: The contractual partners may only disseminate copies of information, data and data storage m edia to sub -contractors with the prior approval of an authorized signatory of the other contractual partner.
perturbation.changed_text: The contractual partners can disseminate copies of information, data and data storage media to sub-contractors without any prior approval, at their own discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the original text to remove the requirement for prior approval to disseminate the content to sub-contractors creates an in-text contradiction due to now stating that sub-contractors are free to distribute as they please. This weakens the control over the flow of information and contradicts the general duty of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Restriction on access and dissemination The contractual partners shall restrict the distribution of and access to information they exchange to those persons who need the information to reasonably c arry out the planned activity. They shall ensure that access to this information, including information in electronic form in particular, is adequately protected. The contractual partners can disseminate copies of information, data and data storage media to sub-contractors without any prior approval, at their own discretion.
perturbation.justification: The original text required prior approval for disseminating information to sub-contractors. The changed text removes this requirement, allowing dissemination 'without any prior approval, at their own discretion.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the contractual partners 'shall restrict the distribution of and access to information' and 'ensure that access to this information...is adequately protected.' The change removes a key control mechanism (prior approval), making it impossible to ensure restricted access as mandated by the contradicted text. The changed text creates an in-text contradiction because it allows dissemination without restriction, which inherently conflicts with the requirement to restrict and protect access to information.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall bear any costs that it generates or incurs in relation to the conclusion and execution of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Bomatec shall bear all costs that are generated or incurred in relation to the conclusion and execution of this Agreement, including the Business Partner's costs.
perturbation.explanation: The change makes Bomatec responsible for all costs, including the Business Partner's costs, contradicting the original statement where each party bears their own costs. This contradiction introduces financial ambiguity into the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Costs Bomatec shall bear all costs that are generated or incurred in relation to the conclusion and execution of this Agreement, including the Business Partner's costs.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each party bears its own costs. The changed text shifts the responsibility for all costs, including the Business Partner's costs, to Bomatec. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, re-emphasizing that Bomatec shall bear all costs. This directly contradicts the initial agreement that each party is responsible for their own costs, creating a financial ambiguity and a strong contradiction.
